zotero.abstractNote: In this paper, we present a robust and reliable real-time ball tracking system for robot table tennis. The system is designed to operate in a high-speed environment where the ball moves at velocities of up to 10 m/s. We propose a multi-stage approach that combines a fast, heuristic-based detection method with a robust, model-based tracking algorithm. The detection stage uses a combination of background subtraction and color-based filtering to identify potential ball candidates. The tracking stage employs a Kalman filter to estimate the ball's state and a data association algorithm to handle occlusions and false positives. We evaluate our system on a real robot table tennis setup and demonstrate that it can track the ball with high accuracy and reliability in real-time.
